one_liner: INTC at $112.54 — Parkev's DCF fair value is $51 even after yesterday's 10% sell-off; Grade D fundamentals, negative MACD, and earnings July 23 create a high-risk binary; avoid.

price_action.vol_rank: Below average — modest volume on day after a 10%+ sell-off
price_action.intraday_story: INTC bounced from yesterday's 10%+ sell-off, opening at $114.87, fading to $111.51, then recovering to close at $112.54 below the open — a weak recovery candle. Volume below average is not encouraging for a bounce.

news_catalyst.headline: DeepSeek Chip Threat and Samsung AI Spending Concerns Sent INTC Down 10%+ Yesterday; Bouncing Today
news_catalyst.summary: INTC was the worst performer in yesterday's semiconductor sell-off on DeepSeek and Samsung concerns. Today's 2% bounce is a dead-cat pattern — weak volume recovery after a large decline. Parkev Tatevosian's DCF shows $51 fair value vs $112.54 current price. Earnings July 23 are the next binary — any further demand weakness would be devastating.

technical.summary: RSI 43.6 approaching oversold. MACD -2.932 is negative and expanding bearishly. Below EMA50 ($116.39) and barely above EMA200 ($110.18). Parkev's DCF at $51 (vs $112) means the stock remains overvalued at current prices even after the sell-off. Phase B Wyckoff classification may reflect the base structure but fundamentals don't support accumulation.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Wyckoff Phase B with bullish bias conflicts with the fundamental overvaluation. Avoid — any technical bounce is a selling opportunity until DCF gap closes.

valuation.summary: DCF not feasible per backend model. Parkev's independent calculation: fair value $51 vs $112.54 current price — 55% overvalued. INTC has been losing CPU market share to AMD and ARM; the AI data center opportunity is real but unproven.

key_strengths: 18A process node could restore manufacturing competitiveness in 2027
key_concerns: Grade D (0.382) — worst quartile on watchlist
Losing CPU market share to AMD and ARM
Earnings July 23 — consensus expect continued losses
fundamentals.summary: Grade D reflects Intel's ongoing transition struggles. The $51 DCF fair value vs $112 current price means even after the DeepSeek-driven sell-off, the stock remains significantly overvalued.

risks: Earnings July 23 — any miss on gross margin recovery or data center win commentary would target EMA200 ($110) and below
Parkev's $51 DCF fair value means 55% downside from current price even at today's levels
Dead-cat bounce pattern today — below-average volume recovery after 10%+ decline is bearish
